title: dbt Deployment & CI/CD Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of scheduling, testing, and continuous deployment in dbt.

questions.question: Which specific file must dbt compare its current state against to enable 'Slim CI' (running only changed models)?

options.text: run_results.json
dbt_project.yml
profiles.yml
manifest.json

questions.question: What does the `--defer` flag do in a dbt run?

options.text: Defers execution until the database has no other active queries.
Resolves `ref()` statements for unchanged upstream models to point to their production counterparts.
Skips materialization of tables and only checks for SQL syntax errors.
Runs tests before building the models.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the difference between dbt Core and dbt Cloud?

options.text: dbt Core is a command-line tool, while dbt Cloud adds an IDE, job scheduler, and hosted documentation.
dbt Core only supports Snowflake, while dbt Cloud supports BigQuery and Redshift.
dbt Cloud provides a data warehouse engine to run queries without external databases.
dbt Core requires Python, while dbt Cloud is built entirely in Node.js.

questions.question: To generate the static documentation website for a dbt project, which command is used?

options.text: dbt compile
dbt build docs
dbt docs generate
dbt clean

questions.question: When configuring profiles.yml for dbt, what purpose does the `target` configuration serve?

options.text: It specifies the data warehouse connection settings (like dev or prod) to use.
It selects which dbt models will be executed during a run.
It defines the S3 bucket where dbt logs are uploaded.
It scopes the specific users who have permission to trigger the pipeline.
